description: Provisioning lifecycle parity vectors. 10 positive vectors (one per *-observed event family) plus 19 negative vectors (one per validator-emitted stable error code under provisioning.*). Every vector is envelope-accepted (expected.accepted = true). Negative vectors additionally declare expected.errors[] for the provisioning-lifecycle extension validator. The wire-envelope canonical-truth test in @peac/protocol filters these dotted extension-level codes; the schema-validator corpus test in @peac/schema reads them directly. Two stable codes are not exercised by corpus vectors: provisioning.invalid_utf8 (fixture-loader-only) and provisioning.structure_too_deep (kernel and extension walker share the same depth cap; round-tripping through parity-vector comparison is path-sensitive and brittle). Both are covered by schema unit tests.

vectors.description: Positive: provisioning-catalog-observed; caller observed catalog discovery for a managed-database service.
Positive: provisioning-provider-link-observed; caller observed an account-to-provider link.
Positive: provisioning-account-observed; caller observed an account being created.
Positive: provisioning-resource-observed; caller observed a managed-database resource being provisioned.
Positive: provisioning-credential-observed; caller observed a credential being issued and stored in an external secret store.
Positive: provisioning-payment-authorization-observed; caller observed a card-credit payment authorization being granted.
Positive: provisioning-budget-observed; caller observed a tenant budget being established.
Positive: provisioning-subscription-observed; caller observed a subscription being started.
Positive: provisioning-domain-observed; caller observed a domain being registered.
Positive: provisioning-deployment-observed; caller observed a deployment being completed.
Negative (schema-level): forbidden top-level credential-bearing key 'token' present at the extension top level.
Negative (schema-level): account_ref contains whitespace and no recognized opaque-ref prefix.
Negative (schema-level): a JWT-compact token literal embedded in a string value triggers the recursive credential-material walker.
Negative (schema-level): nested forbidden credential-bearing key 'token' inside a recognized scope.
Negative (schema-level): storage_surface.kind value is outside the closed enum.
Negative (schema-level): storage_surface.material_redaction value is outside the closed enum.
Negative (schema-level): event_kind value is not one of the 10 registered *-observed kinds.
Negative (schema-level): account.sub_event value is outside the closed enum.
Negative (schema-level): payment_authorization_observation.scheme_id contains characters outside the bounded grammar.
Negative (schema-level): an unrecognized field is present at the extension top level.
Negative (schema-level): max_amount_minor uses leading zeros.
Negative (schema-level): observed_at is not an RFC 3339 timestamp with offset.
Negative (schema-level): catalog.retrieved_at is not an RFC 3339 timestamp with offset.
Negative (schema-level): payment_authorization_observation.expires_at is not an RFC 3339 timestamp with offset.
Negative (schema-level): payment_authorization_observation.currency is not an ISO-4217 uppercase 3-letter code.
Negative (schema-level): a registered string field exceeds the recursive walker default cap of 8192 UTF-8 bytes (still under the kernel MAX_STRING_LENGTH=65536).
Negative (schema-level): a string value contains the U+FFFD replacement character.

Negative (schema-level): total node count exceeds the extension walker default of 10000 (50 sibling arrays of 201 entries each = ~10100 nodes), while staying under the kernel MAX_ARRAY_LENGTH=10000 per-array cap and MAX_TOTAL_NODES=100000.
Negative (schema-level): observed_at is missing at the extension top level.
